IN THE EARLY MORNING HOURS OF WEDNESDAY MORNING MARCH 28, A CLUSTER OF STORMS MOVED EAST ACROSS PORTIONS OF CALDWELL COUNTY. ALONG THE LEADING EDGE OF THE THUNDERSTORMS, A NWS SURVEY CONCLUDED A SMALL EF-0 (EST. PEAK WIND, 80MPH) TORNADO FORMED AND PRODUCED SOME MINOR DAMAGE APPROXIMATELY 7 MILES NORTHEAST OF LULING. WIND DAMAGE TO SOME TREES WERE FOUND ON COUNTY ROAD 144 WITH SOME ROOF DAMAGE REPORTED OFF OF WATTSVILLE ROAD. A SMALL TORNADO TRACK WAS FOUND THAT CROSSED PASTURE ROAD AND CONTINUED EAST JUST NORTH OF WATTSVILLE ROAD.

THE SAN ANTONIO FIRE DEPARTMENT REPORTED 63 HIGH WATER RESCUES ACROSS THE CITY OF SAN ANTONIO IN THE OVERNIGHT HOURS. THERE WAS WIDESPREAD HEAVY RAIN OF 8 TO 10 INCHES, WITH ISOLATED TOTALS NEAR 12 INCHES IN A BROAD AREA OF NORTHERN SAN ANTONIO FROM THE RIM SHOPPING CENTER, EAST TOWARD TPC PARKWAY AND EVANS ROAD. THE HEAVIEST RAIN WAS JUST NORTH OF LOOP 1604. MULTIPLE ROADS AND LOW WATER CROSSINGS WERE FLOODED ALONG WITH SEVERAL AREA CREEKS. SOME ROADS WERE STILL CLOSED FOR THE TUESDAY MORNING COMMUTE.
